sections.paragraph:
fut. יִסְבֹּל to bear, to carry, a heavy burden. (Syr., Ch., id.) Isa 46:4, Isa 46:7 Gen 49:15. Used figuratively to bear griefs, sins, etc. i.e. to receive the penalties which another has deserved, Isa 53:4, 11 Isa 53:11 Lam 5:7. [It must not be forgotten that when the vicarious sufferings of Christ are spoken of, every figure falls very far short of the full truth; he actually bore our sins.]

Pual, part. laden sc. with young, hence pregnant, gravid, used of cattle, Psa 144:14. Compare Arab. حَامِلُ carrying, bearing in the womb, ثَقُلَ, to be pregnant, to bear in the womb. Syr. ܛܥܝܺܢܐܳ laden, gravid.

Hithpael הִסְתַּבֵּל to become burdensome, Ecc 12:5. Hence the four following nouns.
